channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India May Develop Its Own 6th Generation Fighter Alongside Possible FCAS/GCAP Participation: Sources
item.description: India’s future combat aviation roadmap could include two parallel sixth-generation fighter paths, as discussions continue with France over potential participation in the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) or Global Combat Air Programme program. According to sources familiar with the discussions, India may not limit itself to joining the European initiative and could also fund development of its own sixth-generation combat aircraft platform tailored to its long-term operational requirements.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL and GE Accelerate F414 Engine Local Production Timeline to Two Years, Phase-II by 2030
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) and General Electric (GE) Aerospace have reached a key understanding to compress the setup time for local manufacturing of the F414-GE-INS6 jet engine from the earlier three-year target to just two years. This accelerated timeline will primarily power the LCA Tejas Mk2 fighter and the first two squadrons of the Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) Mk1.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: AMCA Refueling Probe Development Accelerates as Timeline Cut to 36 Months
item.description: India’s Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) programme has registered another key design and development milestone, with a recent vendor meeting finalising an accelerated timeline for the aircraft’s in-flight refueling probe. Initially proposed at 60 months, the development cycle has now been compressed to 36 months from the date of contract, reflecting the urgency attached to the AMCA’s overall induction schedule.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Navy Seeks ‘Rearming by Drone’ System to Reload Missiles at Sea
item.description: The Indian Navy has outlined a transformative concept under the DISC 14 innovation challenge, seeking the development of a “Rearming by Drone at Sea” (REARM-D) capability to enable in-situ replenishment of surface-to-air missiles (SAMs) onboard warships. The initiative addresses a critical operational challenge in modern naval warfare, where high-intensity engagements can rapidly deplete onboard missile inventories.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: 9M311-M1 Missile Upgrade for Indian Army’s Tunguska Platforms to take on Drone threats
item.description: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) has cleared a ?445 crore procurement aimed at strengthening the Indian Army’s short-range air defence capabilities, but contrary to initial assumptions, the deal does not involve the acquisition of new 2K22 Tunguska systems. Instead, the approval focuses on procuring advanced 9M311-M1 surface-to-air missiles to upgrade the Army’s existing Tunguska-M1 platforms.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Arctus Aerospace Showcases AX-1 Long-Endurance UAV for Earth Observation Missions
item.description: Arctus Aerospace has unveiled its Arctus AX-1, an 8-metre class long-endurance unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) designed primarily for earth observation roles, signalling the company’s entry into the medium-altitude surveillance segment with a focus on cost-effective persistence and modular payload integration.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistan Races to Acquire China’s Shaanxi KJ-500 AWACS Nearly One Year After Operation Sindoor Setbacks
item.description: Nearly a year after Operation Sindoor, Pakistan appears to be accelerating efforts to rebuild and modernise its airborne surveillance and command capabilities, with plans to acquire China’s Shaanxi KJ-500 Airborne Warning and Control System (AWACS). The move comes in the backdrop of significant reported losses during the 2025 conflict, where India is believed to have neutralised key Pakistani air assets, including a high-value AWACS platform.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: HAL’s New Helicopter Factory in Tumakuru Gets LCH Production Line
item.description: Secretary of Defence Production Sanjeev Kumar on Monday officially opened the new structure assembly line for the Prachand Light Combat Helicopter (LCH) at the Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) factory in Tumakuru, Karnataka.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India and France conduct comprehensive review of defence and nuclear cooperation
item.description: India and France on Monday carried out a comprehensive review of their bilateral ties in areas of civil nuclear energy, defence, space and digital cooperation against the backdrop of the West Asia conflict. In their talks in Paris, Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri and Secretary-General of the French Foreign Ministry Martin Briens also exchanged views on the evolving situation in West Asia.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Make In India Boost: GE Aerospace, IAF To Set Up Engine Repair Facility In India For Tejas Fleet
item.description: In a boost to Make in India, US-based aircraft engine maker GE Aerospace on Monday announced a new agreement with the Indian Air Force to set up an in-country depot facility for F404-IN20 engines that power the HAL Tejas fighter jets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: DRDO to showcase Pralay, BrahMos missiles, other advance defence systems at Bihar exhibition
item.description: India’s cutting-edge defence technologies, including the BrahMos and Akash missile systems, Main Battle Tank Arjun and the Pinaka Multiple Launch Rocket System, will be showcased at a mega exhibition in Bihar from April 15 to 18, officials said on Monday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Iran denies charging toll for Indian tankers transiting Hormuz strait
item.description: Indian tankers that have transited through the Strait of Hormuz did not pay tolls to Iran, Tehran’s ambassador to New Delhi said on Monday, after U.S. President Donald Trump vowed to block passage for vessels making payments to Iran.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ‘Homage to valiant souls’: Defence Ministry pays tribute to Op Meghdoot heroes on Siachen Day
item.description: On the occasion of Siachen Day, the Ministry of Defence on Monday paid tribute to the unwavering courage and commitment of soldiers guarding the icy heights of the world’s highest battlefield. On April 13, 1984, the Indian Army mounted the secretly planned Operation Meghdoot and took control of the Siachen Glacier.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Manipur: 3 militants arrested, illegal bunkers dismantled ?
item.description: Security forces on Monday arrested three militants belonging to different insurgent outfits and dismantled four illegally constructed bunkers in separate operations, officials said.? A police official said that Manipur Police, assisted by Assam Police, arrested a militant belonging to the People’s Liberation Army and its sister outfit, Revolutionary People’s Front, from Rupnagar in Hojai district of Assam.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Lucknow NIA court sentences three Al-Qaeda-linked terrorists to life imprisonment
item.description: A court in Lucknow on Monday sentenced three terrorists linked to the Al-Qaeda-affiliated terror module Ansar Ghazwat-ul-Hind (AGH) to life imprisonment for conspiring to carry out serial blasts in Uttar Pradesh and other parts of the country.
item.dc:creator: admin
